title: anplectrum divaricatum var. anceps
intro: Anplectrum divaricatum var. anceps, also known as Aplectrum var. anceps or Adder's tongue orchid, is a rare, terrestrial orchid species native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain alkaloids that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Anplectrum divaricatum var. anceps include its potential use in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, while also being used as a natural remedy for sore throats and wounds. Herbal preparations of Anplectrum divaricatum var. anceps typically involve the roots being dried and made into teas or infusions, or being combined with other herbs to create topical salves and ointments.
